7702.history: HMS Bayonet (T/Boom Skipper A. Lamont, RNR) was mined and sunk while working on the boom in South Inchkeith Channel off Leith in the Firth of Forth, in position 56º00'N, 03º10'W, on 21 December 1939. Three men were lost and 28 injured. The mine was from a defensive British field laid by the British minelayer HMS Plover between 14 and 16 December 1939.
commanders: A. Lamont, RNR (3 Sep 1939 - 21 Dec 1939)

7703.history: Stricken on 28 March 1946. Sold on 3 April 1947.

7750.history: The Italian submarine Galileo Galilei was stationed in the Red Sea in June 1940. While on patrol her air-conditioning unit malfunctioned and she was forced to the surface where she was engaged by British warships until she was captured south of Aden in position 12º48'N, 45º12'E by the British ASW trawler HMS Moonstone. She was named X 2 by the Royal Navy and in June 1942 she was renamed P 711. She was used as a training boat.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. (retired) Patrick Ernest Heathfield, RN (22 Sep 1940 - Ca. late41)

7751.history: Commissioned as German U-570 on 15 May 1941. The German submarine U-570 was captured by Britain on 27 August 1941 in the North Atlantic south of Iceland, in position 62.15N, 18.35W, after being damaged by a British Hudson aircraft (269 Sqn RAF/S). She was towed to Thorlakshafn, Iceland and salvaged. She was commissioned into the Royal Navy as HMS Graph on 5 October 1941. HMS Graph was decommissioned from active service on 21 June 1943 when she was paid off into dockyard control at the Chatham Dockyard. The submarine was lost on 20 March 1944 when she ran aground on the west coast of Islay, Scotland while en-route to be scrapped. Wreck broken up in 1961.
commanders: Lt. George Robson Colvin, RN (31 Aug 1941 - 10 Oct 1941)
Lt. Edward Dudley Norman, DSO, DSC, RN (10 Oct 1941 - 1 Jun 1942)
Lt. Peter Barnsley Marriott, RN (1 Jun 1942 - 2 Apr 1943)
Lt. David Swanston, DSC, RN (3 Apr 1943 - 21 Jun 1943)

7752.history: On 9 July 1942 the Italian submarine Perla (commissioned into the Italian Navy on 8 July 1936) was captured off Beirut by the British corvette HMS Hyacinth. She was commissioned into the Royal Navy as HMS P 712. On 5 December 1942 she was transferred to the Greek Navy. She served as Matrozos until decommissioned in 1954.

7753.history: On 9 July 1942 the Italian submarine Perla (commissioned into the Italian Navy on 8 July 1936) was captured off Beirut by the British corvette HMS Hyacinth. She was commissioned into the Royal Navy as HMS P 712. On 5 December 1942 she was transferred to the Greek Navy. She served as Matrozos until decommissioned in 1954.

7754.history: On 12 July 1943 the Italian submarine Bronzo was captured off Syracuse by HMS Boston, HMS Cromarty, HMS Poole and HMS Seaham. She was commissioned into the Royal Navy as HMS P 714. On 29 January 1944 she was transferred to the Free French. She served as Narval until 1949.

7755.history: On 12 July 1943 the Italian submarine Bronzo was captured off Syracuse by HMS Boston, HMS Cromarty, HMS Poole and HMS Seaham. She was commissioned into the Royal Navy as HMS P 714. On 29 January 1944 she was transferred to the Free French. She was commissioned on 20 February 1944. She served as Narval until 1949.

7756.history: HMS Veldt was transferred to the Greek Navy upon completion on 1 November 1943. She was renamed Pipinos. Pipinos was returned to the Royal Navy on 10 December 1957. Scrapped at Dunston on 23 February 1958.

7757.history: HMS Vengeful was lent to the Greek Navy on 23 April 1945 until 1957. Scrapped at Gateshead on 22 March 1958.
commanders: Lt. Anthony Stuart Melville-Ross, DSC, RN (17 Jul 1944 - 23 Apr 1945)

7758.history: HMS Vineyard was transferred to the Free French Navy upon completion on 1 August 1944. She was renamed Doris. Doris was returned to the Royal Navy on 18 November 1947. Scrapped at Charlestown in June 1950.

7759.history: HMS Vortex was transferred to the Free French Navy upon completion on 1 December 1944. She was renamed Morse. Morse was returned to the Royal Navy on 17 September 1946. In 1947 she was loaned to the Danish Navy as Saelen. Saelen was returned to the Royal Navy on 16 January 1958. Scrapped at Faslane in August 1958.

7760.history: HMS Variance was transferred to the Royal Norwegian Navy upon completion on 24 August 1944. She was renamed Utsira. Utsira was scrapped at Hamburg in 1965.
commanders: Lt. Sigurd Valvatne, RNON (Aug 1944 - )

7761.history: The Turkish submarine Oruc Reis was commissioned into the Royal Navy for passage to Turkey on 1 December 1941. On 26 March 1942 she left the Clyde for Gibraltar. On April 7th she left Gibraltar for Alexandria where she arrived on April 25th. She arrived at the Turkish naval base at Iskauderun on 9 May 1942 and was handed over to the Turkish Navy.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Walter Selby Hall, RN (8 Aug 1941 - Sep 41 ?)
Lt. Paul Seymour Skelton, RN (Nov 1941 - 9 May 1942)

7762.history: She left the Clyde for Turkey on 25 March 1942. She was handed over to the Turkish Navy on 25 May 1942.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Walter Selby Hall, RN (Oct 41 ? - Nov 41 ?)
Lt. Arthur John Wright Pitt, RN (Dec 1941 - 25 May 1942)

7763.history: Taken over by the Royal Navy during construction. Transferred to the control of the Turkish Government on 9 May 1945
commanders: Lt. Denis John Beckley, RN (Jan 1942 - Sep 1942)
Lt. Hartley Withers Wilkinson, RN (Sep 1942 - late 1943)
Lt. Edmund Roger Stone, RN (27 Jun 1944 - 19 Jul 1944)
T/Lt. Percy Clive Stanbury Pritchard, RNR (19 Jul 1944 - 17 Feb 1945)
Lt. John Francis Michell, RN (17 Feb 1945 - 9 May 1945)

7764.history: Taken over by the Royal Navy during construction. HMS P 615 (Lt. Charles Walderne St Clair Lambert, DSC and Bar, RN) was sunk on 18 April 1943 about 100 nautical miles south-west of Freetown in position 06º49'N, 13º09'W by a torpedo from the German submarine U-123. All hands were lost. Hit by U-boatSunk on 18 Apr 1943 by U-123 (Schroeter).
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Walter Selby Hall, RN (early 1942 - 13 Apr 1942)
Lt. Peter Edward Newstead, RN (13 Apr 1942 - 19 Oct 1942)
Lt. Charles Walderne St Clair Lambert, DSC, RN (19 Oct 1942 - 18 Apr 1943 (+))

7776.history: During the evening of 15 August 1940, 11 Italian S.79 (6 bombers, 5 torpedo-armed) attacked the Mediterranean Fleet in Alexandria harbor. The torpedo planes attack failed completely but the bombers, belonging to the 10º Stormo and led personally by Col. Giovanni Benedetti, charged with creating a diversion, sank the Moorstone in shallow water. The ship was later raised and repaired. One member of the crew was killed in the attack.

7789.history: Laid down as USS Corbesier (DE 106). Transferred to the Free French upon completion. Renamed Yser in 1962. Decommissioned in May 1964. Sold to be broken up for scrap to Walter Ritscher, Hamburg (Germany) on 18 October 1965.

7790.history: Laid down as USS Cronin (DE 107). Transferred to the Free French upon completion. Renamed Oise in 1962. Decommissioned in May 1965. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 11 November 1965.

7791.history: Laid down as USS Crosley (DE 108). Transferred to the Free French upon completion. Decommissioned in May 1964. Sold to be broken up for scrap.

7792.history: Laid down as USS Marocain (DE 109). Transferred to the Free French upon completion. Decommissioned in May 1964. Sold to be broken up for scrap.

7793.history: Laid down as USS Hova (DE 110). Transferred to the Free French upon completion. Decommissioned in May 1964. Sold to be broken up for scrap.

7794.history: Laid down as USS Somali (DE 111). Transferred to the Free French upon completion. Disarmed in 1956 and used as an experimental vessel. Renamed Arago in 1968.

7795.history: Served as the Free French corvette Lobelia (name not changed from 1941). Returned to the Royal Navy in April 1947. Sold to Norway on 3 May 1947 for conversion to trader, but sold on to Bryde & Dahl in July 1948. Refitted as bouy-boat, renamed Thorgeir. Last season as whaler was 1966/1967. Laid up in Sandefjord from May 1967 to June 1969 when sold to Norsk Skipsopphugning, Grimstad for breaking up.

7796.history: The French submarine Narval joined the Free French Navy at Malta at the time of the French surrender.Narval was mined north-east of Sfax, Tunisia in position 35º03'N, 11º53'E on or around 15 December 1940.

7797.built_by: Koninklijke Maatschappij De Schelde (Vlissingen (Flushing), The Netherlands)
7797.history: HrMs Z 5 escaped to the U.K. on 14 May 1940. She was transferred to the Royal Navy on 2 March 1942. In May 1943 she was renamed HMS Blade. Returned to the Royal Netherlands Navy on 9 April 1945 but not commissioned. Sold and scrapped at Troon, Scotland in October 1945. More on this ship can be found at this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Willem van Lier, RNN (15 Sep 1939 - 24 Mar 1941)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Pieter Atzo de Boer, RNN (24 Mar 1941 - 18 May 1941)
luitenant ter zee 2e klasse (Lt.) Jan Julius Steensma, RNN (18 May 1941 - 25 Jan 1942)
luitenant ter zee 2e klasse (Lt.) Johannes Leonardus Bouman, RNN(R) (25 Jan 1942 - 2 Mar 1942)

7798.built_by: Koninklijke Maatschappij De Schelde (Vlissingen (Flushing), The Netherlands)
7798.history: HrMs Z 6 escaped to the U.K. on 14 May 1940. She did some escort work before being decommissioned on 4 October 1940. Sold to be broken up for scrap in March 1942. Scrapped at Bo'ness in February 1943. More on this ship can be found at this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Adrianus Cornelis Lunbeck, RNN (7 Jul 1939 - 13 Oct 1940)

7799.history: HrMs Z 7 escaped to the U.K. on 14 May 1940. Decommisioned on 16 July 1942. Transferred to the Royal Navy on 1 October 1942. Decommissioned by the Royal Navy in 1944. Broken up for scrap Llanelly in 1947. More on this ship can be found at this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Heinrich Witte, RNN (8 Sep 1939 - 20 Jul 1940)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) R Rijkens, RNN(R) (20 Jul 1940 - 14 Aug 1940)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Nicolaas Wilhelm Sluijter, RNN (14 Aug 1940 - 6 Jan 1941)

7800.history: HrMs Z 8 escaped to the U.K. on 14 May 1940. Decommissioned on 16 January 1942. Transferred to the Royal Navy on 1 October 1942. Scrapped at Newport in August 1944. More on this ship can be found at this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Pieter Atzo de Boer, RNN (7 Apr 1939 - 24 Mar 1941)

7801.history: HrMs Z 3 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. Goedhart) was scuttled by her own crew, to prevent capture by the Germans, on the IJsselmeer off Enkhuizen on 14 May 1940. Raised and scrapped in 1941. More on this ship can be found at this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Gerardus Cornelis Voorrips, RNN(R) (5 Jan 1940 - 30 Mar 1940)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Johannes Frans Goedhart, RNN(R) (30 Mar 1940 - 14 May 1940)

7802.built_by: Nederlands-Indische Scheepsbouw en Kustvaart Maatschappij (Lasem, Netherlands East Indies)
7802.history: Commissioned as auxiliary minelayer on 16 February 1942. Scuttled by her own crew at Soerabaya on 2 March 1942 to prevent her capture by the Japanese. More on this ship see this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 2e klasse (Lt.) Herman Nicolaas Sax, RNN(R) ( - 2 Mar 1942)

7803.built_by: Soerabaya Droogdok Maatschappij (Soerabaya, Netherlands East Indies)
7803.history: Commissioned as auxiliary minelayer on 17 June 1940. Scuttled by her own crew at Soerabaya on 2 March 1942 to prevent her capture by the Japanese. More on this ship can be found at this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 2e klasse (Lt.) Tobias Jellema, RNN (14 Jun 1940 - 2 Mar 1942)

7804.built_by: Koninklijke Maatschappij De Schelde (Vlissingen (Flushing), The Netherlands)
7804.history: Former Dutch submarine HrMs K IX. Intended to be used for ASW training but due to many defects she saw almost no service. Decommissioned on 31 March 1944.
commanders: Lt. Frederick Melbourne Piggott, RNR (22 Jun 1943 - 31 Mar 1944)

7805.history: Used as training sbmarine. Captured by the Germans on 14 May 1940. Commissioned by the Germans as UD-1 on 21 November 1940. Decommissioned by the Germans on 23 November 1943. UD-1 was scuttled by the Germans at Kiel on 3 May 1945.

7806.history: Used as training submarine before the Second World War. Decommissioned on 21 November 1939. Captured by the Germans in May 1940 but not used by them due to her old age. Sunk due to a leak at Den Helder on 2 May 1944.

7807.history: Never commissioned into the Royal Netherlands Navy. Scuttled on 14 May 1940 in the Nieuwe Waterweg at Rotterdam to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Germans and towed to Blohm & Voss at Hamburg. Commissioned as ZH 1 in the Kriegsmarine on 11 October 1942. On 9 June 1944 she was scuttled by her own crew off Ile de Bas, France after being heavily damaged during an action with an Allied destroyer force.
